title: Olearia hygrophila
taxonomy: Olearia hygrophila is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to New Zealand. The plant is classified in the genus Olearia, which includes over 200 species of shrubs and small trees. Olearia hygrophila is often referred to as a wetland daisy or marsh daisy. The classification places it within the larger group of daisy-like plants that are commonly found in temperate regions.

intro: Olearia hygrophila, also known as Swamp Daisy or Swamp Olearia, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in Western Australian Aboriginal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and sore throats, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Olearia hygrophila include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Olearia hygrophila include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory infections and other inflammatory conditions.
